web.title: Visiting France In Summer - Best Summer Holiday Destinations: 2026 ...
web.description: From the French Riviera to the city of Bordeaux, it’s time to visit France in summer with this guide to France's best summer holiday destinations.
snippets: Whether you fancy relaxing on the beach, exploring towns and cities, eating great French food or getting active, there’s plenty to keep everyone happy. Here are a few of my top recommendations of places to visit in France in the summer months. I found Nice to be a great city to explore in the warmer months. Take a walk along the Promenade Des Anglais which goes along the seafront for several kilometres, passing beach clubs, smart restaurants and sunbathing spots.

Take a stroll along the river, check out cute vintage stores, eat at a food stall and relax with a glass of vin rouge – sounds like the perfect place to visit in France in summer to me! ... Right on the border with Italy, Menton feels world’s away from Nice and Cannes along the French Riviera. It has its own micro-climate, is famous for its lemons… plus there’s a benefit to being so close to Italy – you can hop across the border for some fresh Italian produce! If you’re looking to make your holiday extra special, Menton is home to Mirazur – one of the best restaurants in the world.

Where to spend summer in France with a family? Try Trégastel – one of the prettiest beaches in Brittany in Northern France. The coastline is famous for its pink granite rocks. Not far away is Chateau de Costaeres – a beautiful pink granite castle built on a rock. ... We all know Cannes for its famous film festival, but it’s a great holiday spot too, with plenty of chic villas, smart restaurants and a port filled with swanky boats. Take a walk along La Croisette – a palm tree lined promenade sometimes referred to as the Cannes Catwalk, and you’ll feel a million dollars!
There are so many wonderful places to visit in France in summer – I really hope this has helped you decide where to go on your summer holiday!

web.title: Plan your Summer Holiday in France - Eurotunnel LeShuttle™
web.description: Spend summer in France! Discover the best French holiday destinations in this handy guide, from the sun-kissed south to beautiful Brittany. Book a trip today!
snippets: Spend summer in Antibes for a relaxing coastal break · Nestled between glitzy Nice and Cannes, the resort town of Antibes is known for multi-million-dollar mega yachts and its maze-like Old Town encased by 16th-century ramparts. Artists such as Max Ernst and Pablo Picasso have tried to capture the beauty of Antibes in their works, there’s even a museum dedicated to Picasso and his life there to visit while you’re here. Spend your summer holiday lazing on the beautiful beaches, or take a stroll into the town to marvel at the architecture and visit the local markets.

The summer season can be busy, so book your accommodation and tickets to popular attractions in advance. This will help to avoid disappointment and ensure you benefit from the best prices. If you want to skip the crowds, consider booking your holiday for late June or early September for a quieter break. Check French driving rules and regulations for a smooth and stress-free trip. And lastly, bring plenty of sunscreen. Whether you are relaxing at the beach, enjoying a theme park, or exploring local villages, you’ll be glad you did.

No summer holiday in France feels complete without a mix of beach days, hiking trails, kayaking adventures, vineyards tours and outdoor markets. With so much to do and never enough time to do it all - you’ll find yourself planning your next trip before you drive home. With so many fascinating places to visit in France, it can be hard to know how to narrow down your choices! When deciding where to go, it’s helpful to ask yourself:

If its French countryside you are looking for, this is the place to go. Spend your summer break castle spotting or wine tasting in this part of the country frequently described as ‘The Garden of France’. Particularly popular for cycling holidays, the Loire Valley contains possibly the most popular cycling route through this part of France, the Loire à Vélo.

web.title: A summer off the beaten track in France - Explore France
web.description: Dreaming of an unforgettable summer full of unique experiences? Take a step back in time and relax in a secret spa, cycle the back roads or recharge your batter
snippets: Dreaming of an unforgettable summer full of unique experiences? Take a step back in time and relax in a secret spa, cycle the back roads or recharge your batteries in the heart of an undiscovered green landscape. France is full of hidden paradises, unspoilt landscapes and cultural treasures.

Summer holidays are also the ideal time to relax and pamper yourself. So why not choose a spa holiday for a summer off the beaten track and recharge your batteries before the new school year begins? If you've got a Mediterranean accent, head to Balaruc-les-Bains, France's only seaside resort.

Both relaxing and rejuvenating, France's lakes are the perfect combination for lovers of swimming, water sports and peace and quiet. Ideal for a summer away from the crowds. In Brittany, the Lac de Trémelin is a must for kayakers and canoeists. Set in the heart of the Brocéliande forest, it's also a nature reserve and a great base for walking, horse-riding and cycling.

This summer, answer the call of the calm of rivers and canals. All over France, get on board for soothing river trips and holidays off the beaten track. In the heart of the Nouvelle Aquitaine region, go canoeing on the Leyre. This calm river rises in the heart of the Landes region and winds its way 115 km to the Arcachon basin, just 1 hour from Bordeaux.

web.title: Walking Holidays in France | Ramble Worldwide
web.description: On walking holidays in France, visit fairy-tale Alpine pastures, secret medieval villages and flourishing vineyards. Book now with Ramble Worldwide.
snippets: So, if you want to go it alone but still benefit from our extensive knowledge and carefully planned routes, Ramble Worldwide can make it happen. To see the destinations currently available, take a look at our self-guided walking holidays page. The optimal times for walking in France vary by region. For the Alps and other mountain areas, summer (June-September) offers the best conditions with snow-free paths and pleasant temperatures.

Our Discover holidays particularly focus on blending walking with cultural immersion. Additionally, most holidays include at least one free day where you can pursue your own interests, whether that's relaxing at a local café, visiting museums, or arranging optional excursions.

Usual daily walks - Distance: 4 miles; Ascent/Descent 300m; Terrain: generally good paths and tracks; Walking Time: Half Day or Full Day walks. ... France’s rich and varied terrain makes it an idyllic destination for walkers of all abilities. Enjoy a leisurely holiday in the bejewelled gardens of the French Riviera and Cote d'Azur, or, meander across wild rosemary bushes on the winding coastal trails of Corsica.
Usually, your walk will take up the whole day at a moderate pace. Terrain will often be hilly and paths generally good. Note that on holidays in mountainous areas, expect some rougher paths.
